workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: I need you to process this email thread from Adatum Corporation. I think there's an opportunity here but need to validate.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-27 11:40 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 09:41 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Adatum Corporation

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-07 08:28 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
